question: [File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: gasLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exhaust the shared gasLimitBucket by sending a single request with gas set to the maximum allowed value (15,000,000), causing scaleGas() to consume 1,500 tokens in one shot and blocking all subsequent transactions from being processed for the remainder of the 1-second refill window, effectively delaying block production by 500% or more of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: gasLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the refillGreedy(gasLimit, Duration.ofSeconds(1)) strategy by sending a burst of max-gas requests at the exact start of each 1-second window, draining the entire gasLimitBucket capacity in milliseconds and causing all other users' transactions to be frozen for the rest of that window, resulting in a block delay exceeding 500% of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: rateLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user flood the rateLimitBucket with 500 lightweight requests per second (each consuming 1 token via tryConsume(1)), exhausting the entire capacity(rateLimit) of 500 tokens and causing all subsequent contract call transactions to be rejected with ThrottleException for the remainder of the second, freezing network transaction throughput beyond the 500% block time delay threshold?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: rateLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of SynchronizationStrategy on the rateLimitBucket (unlike gasLimitBucket which uses SynchronizationStrategy.SYNCHRONIZED) to cause race conditions under concurrent load, allowing more than the configured requestsPerSecond tokens to be consumed simultaneously and destabilizing the throttle, leading to unpredictable transaction freezing?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: gasLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user send repeated requests with gas values just above GAS_SCALE_FACTOR (10,000), causing scaleGas() to return 1 token per request, and by sending exactly gasPerSecond/GAS_SCALE_FACTOR requests drain the gasLimitBucket completely, freezing all higher-gas transactions for the rest of the refill window and causing block delays exceeding 500% of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: gasLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the scaleGas() function returning 0 when gas <= GAS_SCALE_FACTOR (10,000) to send unlimited requests with gas=10,000 without consuming any gasLimitBucket tokens, bypassing the gas throttle entirely and flooding the network with zero-gas-cost requests that still consume rateLimitBucket tokens, causing legitimate high-gas transactions to be starved and block production delayed?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: opcodeRateLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exhaust the opcodeRateLimitBucket (default capacity of 1 token, refillGreedy(1, Duration.ofSeconds(1))) with a single request per second, completely blocking all opcode tracing endpoint access for all other users for the entire second, causing a denial-of-service that freezes opcode-dependent transaction analysis and delays block-level transaction processing?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: gasLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that gasLimitBucket uses SynchronizationStrategy.SYNCHRONIZED (a global lock) to cause lock contention by sending many concurrent high-gas requests, forcing all threads to serialize on the synchronized bucket and creating artificial processing delays that push block production time beyond 500% of the average block time?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: rateLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the greedy refill strategy of rateLimitBucket, where refillGreedy(rateLimit, Duration.ofSeconds(1)) refills tokens as fast as possible rather than at a fixed interval, to time requests precisely at refill moments and consume tokens faster than the intended rate, causing other users' transactions to be frozen for extended periods exceeding 500% of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: gasLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user send a single request with gas equal to the full gasPerSecond capacity (7,500,000,000 / 10,000 = 750,000 scaled tokens), completely draining the gasLimitBucket in one call and causing all subsequent gas-consuming transactions to be rejected for the entire 1-second refill window, effectively freezing network transaction processing?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: rateLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user from multiple IP addresses or connections simultaneously exhaust the single shared rateLimitBucket (capacity 500) by coordinating 500 concurrent requests, causing all legitimate users' transactions to be blocked for the remainder of the second and delaying block production by 500% or more of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: gasLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Math.floorDiv(gas, GAS_SCALE_FACTOR) calculation in scaleGas() to craft gas values that result in maximum token consumption per request (e.g., gas = Long.MAX_VALUE / GAS_SCALE_FACTOR * GAS_SCALE_FACTOR), draining the gasLimitBucket in a single call and freezing all subsequent transactions?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: gasLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the gasLimitBucket capacity equals the refill rate (capacity(gasLimit) == refillGreedy(gasLimit, ...)) to perform a burst attack at the start of each second, consuming all 750,000 scaled tokens instantly and causing a complete transaction freeze for the remainder of each second, creating a sustained pattern of block delays?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: rateLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that rateLimitBucket has no SynchronizationStrategy, causing non-atomic token consumption under high concurrency, where multiple threads simultaneously read the same available token count and all proceed, effectively allowing more than 500 requests per second to pass through and overwhelming downstream transaction processing?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: opcodeRateLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user continuously send one opcode request per second to permanently occupy the opcodeRateLimitBucket (capacity=1), ensuring no other user can ever access the opcode endpoint, causing a permanent denial-of-service that freezes opcode-dependent transaction verification and delays block finalization?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: gasLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the interaction between rateLimitBucket and gasLimitBucket checks in ThrottleManagerImpl.throttle() — where rateLimitBucket is checked first — to send 500 low-gas requests that pass the rate check but collectively exhaust the gasLimitBucket, blocking all subsequent high-gas transactions and causing block production delays exceeding 500% of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: gasLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the restore() function's addTokens() call on the gasLimitBucket to overflow the bucket beyond its configured capacity, causing the bucket to hold more tokens than intended and allowing subsequent requests to bypass the gas throttle, leading to uncontrolled transaction processing that destabilizes block timing?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: rateLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the 1-second refill window of refillGreedy(rateLimit, Duration.ofSeconds(1)) by timing requests to arrive just before the window resets, consuming tokens from both the current and next window's refill, effectively doubling their rate and starving other users' transactions for multiple consecutive seconds?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: gasLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user send a stream of requests with gas values just above GAS_SCALE_FACTOR (10,001) to consume exactly 1 scaled token per request, and by sending 750,000 such requests within a second (limited only by the rateLimitBucket at 500 RPS), drain the gasLimitBucket over multiple seconds and cause a sustained transaction freeze exceeding 500% of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: gasLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the SynchronizationStrategy.SYNCHRONIZED on gasLimitBucket to create a thundering herd attack by sending many concurrent requests simultaneously, causing all threads to queue on the synchronized lock and creating a processing backlog that delays transaction execution far beyond the 500% block time threshold?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: rateLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the shared single-instance rateLimitBucket (a Spring singleton bean) to perform a global denial-of-service by exhausting the 500 RPS limit, affecting all users of the web3 API simultaneously and causing a network-wide transaction freeze that delays block production by 500% or more of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: gasLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a request with gas=Long.MAX_VALUE to cause an integer overflow in scaleGas() via Math.floorDiv(gas, GAS_SCALE_FACTOR), producing an unexpectedly large or negative token consumption value that either crashes the bucket or bypasses the gas throttle entirely, leading to uncontrolled transaction processing and block delays?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: opcodeRateLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the opcodeRateLimitBucket's lack of SynchronizationStrategy (unlike gasLimitBucket) to cause race conditions where multiple concurrent opcode requests simultaneously pass the tryConsume(1) check before the token is decremented, allowing more than 1 opcode request per second and overwhelming the heavy opcode processing endpoint?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: gasLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the greedy refill behavior of gasLimitBucket to perform a sustained low-rate attack — sending requests at exactly the refill rate — that keeps the bucket perpetually near-empty, ensuring that any legitimate high-gas transaction always finds insufficient tokens and is rejected, causing a sustained block production delay exceeding 500% of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: rateLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the rateLimitBucket is checked before the gasLimitBucket in ThrottleManagerImpl.throttle(), by sending 500 requests per second that each pass the rate check but carry maximum gas, causing the gasLimitBucket to be drained in the first few requests and all remaining 490+ requests to fail on gas, creating a cascading freeze of network transactions?
